messages.text: Basket 1 is a lidded wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird—round body with a little head and beak sticking out on one side (with a small eye detail). Let me know when you’ve got it.
Basket 2 is a deep, rounded bucket-style wicker basket with a single very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is medium-brown with a tight weave. Confirm when you’ve placed it.
Basket 3 is a rectangular picnic basket with a tall center handle and a hinged lid that’s flipped open to the right side—light tan, tight weave. Let me know when you’ve got it.

Basket 4 is a small boat-shaped oval basket with a whitewashed/light gray weave and a tan rim; it has a single arched handle that looks wrapped in pale material with little reddish bands near where it meets the sides.
Is it the mostly white/gray oval basket with a tan rim and a pale handle that has small reddish wraps where it meets the sides?
Yes—that’s the one: the mostly white/gray oval “boat” basket with the tan rim and the pale wrapped handle with little reddish wraps where it attaches.
Basket 5: a rectangular, boxy wicker basket with a chunky twisted-rope handle arching high, and a darker band right under the top rim. Let me know when you’ve got it placed.
Basket 6: a round lidded wicker basket with a cat face on top—painted eyes and whiskers—and it sits on little feet.
Basket 7: a rectangular tote basket with a single wide arched handle, a green/teal woven body, and a tan/brown rim with a braided band just under the top edge.
Basket 8: a dark gray/black oval wicker basket with a single thick arched handle, a lighter tan band around the bottom, and a lidded top with a strap-like piece across the front.
Basket 9: a tall, straight-sided cylindrical wicker basket (like a small hamper) with an open top and two small loop handles right near the rim.

distinctive_features: animal-shaped basket (looks like a duck/bird)
rounded body with a small head/beak on the right
small colored eye detail
reasoning.discriminative_strategy: Emphasize it’s a lidded basket shaped like a duck/bird (not a normal handled basket); mention the beak/head profile.

ai_reasoning_log.utterance: Basket 1 is a lidded wicker basket shaped like a duck/bird—round body with a little head and beak sticking out on one side (with a small eye detail). Let me know when you’ve got it.

distinctive_features: lidded
shaped like a duck/bird
head and beak protruding
small eye detail
deep rounded bucket shape
very dark (almost black) thick handle
medium-brown body with a tighter weave and a slightly flared rim
reasoning.discriminative_strategy: Emphasize the very dark thick handle and the deep, rounded bucket profile (not shallow/oval).

ai_reasoning_log.utterance: Basket 2 is a deep, rounded bucket-style wicker basket with a single very dark (almost black) thick arched handle; the body is medium-brown with a tight weave. Confirm when you’ve placed it.

deep rounded bucket body
medium-brown tight weave
reasoning.discriminative_question: Is the handle noticeably darker than the basket body (almost black), and is the basket a deep bucket rather than a shallow tray?

distinctive_features: rectangular picnic basket with a hinged lid flipped open to the right
tall central vertical handle rising from the middle
tight, pale tan weave with squared corners
reasoning.discriminative_strategy: Emphasize the open hinged lid on the right and the tall center handle on a rectangular picnic basket.

hinged lid flipped open to the right
tall center handle

whitewashed/light gray weave with tan rim

pale/cloth-wrapped handle with small reddish wraps near the side attachments
